description_long: This is a futuristic, twin-engine airplane with a sleek, angular design and a compact fuselage. The wings are sharply swept back and feature distinctive geometric patterns in brown, white, and metallic tones. Two large jet engines are mounted above the rear fuselage, flanking a central tail boom that extends to a twin vertical stabilizer configuration. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed with a dark canopy, suggesting advanced avionics. The landing gear is not visible, implying it may be retractable and integrated into the body. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a high-performance, possibly military or sci-fi inspired aircraft.
description: A sleek, twin-engine, swept-wing airplane with a futuristic design, geometric patterns, and a forward-enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, forward cockpit canopy, and the wide, swept-back wings with visible engine intakes above the fuselage.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the angular wing shape, engine nacelles above the fuselage, and the twin vertical stabilizers at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin jet engines, the split tail design, and the full wingspan with geometric markings.
description_view_3: Right side view mirroring the left, showing the detailed wing structure, engine placement, and tail configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, sharply swept wings with moderate anhedral angle and no visible winglets; geometric surface markings.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted above the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a central tail boom, forming an H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on design cues.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or tandem seat.
